mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly expands corporate revenue expectations, lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month without requiring additional macroeconomic conditions.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with payroll growth rates exceeding 0.1 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate compress equity duration multiples by raising the risk-free discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 60% of instances where the 3-month rate change exceeds 0.25 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates broad-based demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising housing starts and industrial production fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the unemployment rate signal labor market normalization, which reduces recession risk premiums and supports equity valuations specifically during periods of elevated initial jobless claims.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not systematically increase when unemployment falls during periods where RSAFS exceeds 500,000.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly reflects increased manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher near-term corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly SPX returns show zero correlation with month-over-month changes in the industrial production index over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand, which forces downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and depresses S&P 500 valuations over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of quarters where real GDP growth drops below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with stagnant federal funds rates indicate real interest rate compression, which erodes purchasing power and triggers equity sell-offs over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of accelerating CPI and flat FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Surging retail sales indicate strong consumer spending momentum, which immediately boosts short-term equity sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during periods of low inflation.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not systematically rise following retail sales spikes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.
